question: Rapid diagnostic tests for diagnosing uncomplicated non-falciparum or Plasmodium vivax malaria in endemic countries (a diagnostic-test-accuracy review: in people with suspected non-falciparum / P. vivax malaria, what is the accuracy of rapid diagnostic tests?)
source: CLEF TAR 2018 Task 2 (Testing), content-level qrels — finally-included studies of Cochrane review CD011431.

protocol.seeds: none — proceed under the no-seed workflow
protocol.framework: diagnostic (PIRD / diagnostic PICO). Index test = rapid diagnostic tests (RDTs); target condition = non-falciparum / Plasmodium vivax malaria.
protocol.essential_concepts: malaria; rapid diagnostic test (RDT). Decide essential block structure yourself per the skill's anti-pattern guidance (prefer fewer AND blocks).
protocol.optional_blocks: DECLINE all optional secondary AND blocks — do NOT add a reference-standard / diagnostic-accuracy block, do NOT add a separate 'endemic countries' geographic block, and do NOT require a non-falciparum/species AND block (handle species scope at screening to protect recall).
protocol.limits: none (no date, language, species, or age limits)
protocol.final_cleanup: remove exact duplicate terms and genuinely zero-hit phrases (after ruling out typos); keep all recall-bearing terms; apply automatically without asking.

notes: Diagnostic-test-accuracy review. Gold set = 26 included studies. No hand-built baseline strategy: use evals/generate.py (Phase 2) to have the skill build one. Some gold studies may predate or fall outside PubMed; the harness reports reachable vs. unreachable separately.
